Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that 1 Identifies cases that have the sequence Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order in direct succession no other events from the same case in between 2 Among those cases further filter to only those where the time between Create Order and Confirm Order is more than 5 days 3 Exclude all events from those filtered cases cases that meet both the sequence AND timing condition from the final result 4 Return all events from cases that do NOT meet both criteria

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Let me think about how to implement this in DuckDB

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: To find direct succession no other events in between I need to use the LEAD window function For each event I look at the next event in the same case ordered by timestamp and check if the current activity and the next two activities match the sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I need the timestamp of the Create Order event and the timestamp of the Confirm Order event The Create Order is the next timestamp from above and Confirm Order is the next next timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 3 Exclude those cases and return all events

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Actually I should be careful with the LEAD function If there are duplicate timestamps the ordering might be ambiguous But typically we assume timestamp is sufficient to determine order or there could be a tiebreaker needed Ill use timestamp as the ordering but the user might want to add another column For now lets stick with timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also I should make sure the intervals are calculated correctly In DuckDB you can use INTERVAL 5 DAY or simply subtract timestamps and compare to an interval

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This should work Let me verify 1 The sequence_check CTE uses LEAD to look at the next two events for each row in each case 2 The cases_to_exclude CTE identifies cases where we find the exact sequence AND the time difference between the Create Order and Confirm Order is more than 5 days 3 The final SELECT returns all events from event_log except those belonging to the identified cases

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: One thing to consider What if a case has multiple instances of this sequence The query would still identify the case DISTINCT and we exclude all events from that case That seems correct based on the prompt

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: What if the timestamps have ties The LEAD might not be deterministic But assuming timestamps are unique within a case or theres some implicit ordering this should be fine The user might need to add a secondary sort key but thats not specified
